title: Tests and Measurements
description: Theory and practice of psychological measurement, statistical models for items and tests, assessment of test reliability and validity, factor analysis, scoring.

title: Brain and Behaviour
description: The neurobiological bases of behaviour; brain processes involved in perception, motivation, emotion, psychopathology, learning and memory.

title: Personality Psychology
description: Theory and research on individual differences in motivation, emotion, and social behaviour.

title: Personality Psychology
description: Theory and research on individual differences in motivation, emotion, and social behaviour.

title: Health Psychology
description: Health-related behaviours such as smoking and drug use; effects of stressful events on health; methods for coping with stress.

title: Socialization: Media Content and Effects
description: Examines human development in the context of the socializing role of media.

title: Advanced Research Methods in Behavioural Sciences
description: Prepares students for further research through experimental design and statistical techniques, with lab work.

title: Clinical Psychology
description: Theoretical and research foundations of the processes of assessment and behaviour modification in clinical psychology.

title: Research in Psychological Disorders
description: Focuses on the cognitive, biological, and motivational bases of psychological disorders, with an emphasis on current research methods.

title: Living Language: Science and Society
description: A multidisciplinary course examining, integrating, and applying subject-specific knowledge through the lens of language and the language sciences framework, focusing on themes of real-world importance.

title: Analysis of Behavioural Data
description: Inferential statistics in psychology and conceptual interpretation of data; experimental design.

title: Research Methods
description: Various research methods used in psychology, with an emphasis on critical thinking and experimental design.

title: Introductory Psychology
description: Introduction to methods and statistics, biopsychology, learning, perception, memory, cognition, motivation, assessment, developmental, personality, clinical, and social psychology.

title: Introduction to Developmental, Social, Personality, and Clinical Psychology
description: Introduction to methods and statistics, motivation, assessment, developmental, personality, clinical, and social psychology.
